# Spec: YAML scalar normalizer

## Intent

Frontmatter and config scalar reads must normalize a raw YAML scalar (quote-aware comment
strip, then dequote a balanced pair, then trim) so a quoted `status: "ready"`/`"draft"` no
longer leaks its quotes and an inline `# …` comment in `.swarm/config.yaml` no longer corrupts
adapter resolution.

## Non-goals

- Not a change to the frozen checks contract (checks.yaml, severities, IDs) — a parser fix only.
- Not list parsing or the list-narrower `scalar()` helpers.

## Requirements

### AC-001 — quoted status enum is honored

When a frontmatter `status` value carries surrounding quotes, the enum guards (C007 blocking on
a ready spec, C012/C013 draft guard) must read the unquoted value.

Verify with: `pnpm run test:run` (the yamlScalar + checkSpec regression specs)

### AC-002 — inline config comments are stripped

When a `.swarm/config.yaml` value carries an inline `# …` comment, adapter resolution must read
the value without the comment (`default: claude # …` resolves to `claude`).

Verify with: `pnpm run test:run` (the agentConfig regression spec)

## Open questions

- None (non-blocking).

## Affected areas

- `src/infra/yamlScalar.ts`
- `src/modules/Core/services/readFrontmatter.ts`
- `src/modules/Core/services/agentConfig.ts`
- `src/modules/Core/services/parseReviewPacket.ts`
- `src/modules/Sol/useCases/parseSpecRecord.ts`
- `src/modules/Sol/useCases/parseChangePlan.ts`

# Task: Wave 1 — the YAML scalar normalizer (#33 config comments + #38 quoted status)

## Source

- Spec: `specs/yaml-scalar/spec.md` (SPEC-yaml-scalar)

## Scope

- **GH-38 (MAJOR)** — a quoted frontmatter `status: "ready"` / `"draft"` leaks the quotes, so the
  C007 (`status !== 'ready'`) and C012/C013 (`status === 'draft'`) enum guards mis-fire — C007
  blocking is *suppressed* on a ready spec, the draft guard is bypassed.
- **GH-33 (MAJOR)** — a valid `.swarm/config.yaml` value with an inline `# …` comment corrupts
  adapter resolution (`default: claude # …` → "unknown agent").

## Do not change

- The frozen checks contract (`checks.yaml`, severities, check IDs) — this is a parser fix only.
- The two list-narrower `scalar()` helpers in `checkReviewFile.ts` / `deriveBoard.ts` (they return
  `value[0]`; they are NOT dequoters — do not fold them into the new helper).
- Reconcile semantics / verdict boundary (ADR-0077 D8).

## Affected areas

- `src/modules/Core/services/yamlScalar.ts` (new)
- `src/modules/Core/services/readFrontmatter.ts`
- `src/modules/Core/services/agentConfig.ts`
- `src/modules/Core/services/parseReviewPacket.ts`
- `src/modules/Sol/useCases/parseSpecRecord.ts`
- `src/modules/Sol/useCases/parseChangePlan.ts`

## Verify

- [ ] `pnpm run typecheck` (no type regressions)
- [ ] `pnpm run test:run` (577 baseline + new specs green) (GH-33, GH-38)
- [ ] `pnpm run lint && pnpm run deps:validate` (boundaries/hygiene)
- [ ] new `yamlScalar.spec.ts` — comment-strip honors quote spans + the `SPEC-x#AC` no-space case; dequote balanced pairs (GH-33, GH-38)
- [ ] new end-to-end specs — `status: "ready"` + TBD → C007 still blocks; `default: claude # c` resolves to `claude` (GH-38, GH-33)

## Agent instructions

1. Build `normalize_scalar(raw)`: comment-strip (quote-aware, YAML `#`-after-whitespace rule),
   then dequote a balanced pair, then the existing trim. Route the scalar frontmatter readers +
   the config value branch through it. Do not touch list parsing or the list-narrowers.
2. Stay in scope; paste real Verify output.
3. Re-read the diff as a skeptic before handing off.

## Run summary

- Changed files: `src/infra/yamlScalar.ts` (+ spec), the 5 readers
  (readFrontmatter, agentConfig, parseReviewPacket, parseSpecRecord, parseChangePlan),
  + regression tests in checkSpec/agentConfig specs. Landed swarm-cli `4ccdf79`.
- Verify results: `typecheck` 0 · `test:run` 0 (586 pass, 577 baseline + 9 new) ·
  `lint` 0 · `deps:validate` 0. #38 red→green proven (without the parser fix:
  "expected 'clean' to be 'blocking'"). Independent review REVIEW-remediation-w1: pass.
- Out-of-scope edits: none. (Helper placed in `src/infra/` not `Core/services/` — a
  layering correction: Core imports Sol, so Sol cannot import Core; reviewer confirmed.)
- Blocked questions: none.
